name: Cloudflare R2 API
what_it_does: Cloudflare R2 — S3-compatible object storage with zero egress fees, accessible via S3 API or Workers runtime, ideal for storing agent-generated files, model outputs, and assets at the edge.
use_cases: Agents storing and retrieving large files (PDFs, images, model outputs) without paying per-GB egress fees
Agent artifact storage — persisting job outputs, logs, and reports accessible globally via R2 public buckets
Workers integration — agents running on Cloudflare Workers reading/writing R2 buckets natively without SDK
Media and asset hosting — agents uploading user-generated content and serving via Cloudflare's edge CDN
Data lake storage — agents using R2 as cost-effective raw data storage with S3-compatible query tools (DuckDB, Athena)
not_for: Transactional storage requiring strong consistency — use PostgreSQL or DynamoDB for ACID guarantees
Very small files at high frequency — per-operation charges make R2 expensive for tiny frequent writes
Teams needing advanced S3 features — S3 Intelligent Tiering, S3 Object Lambda not available in R2
best_when: Your agent generates files that need to be served globally or stored long-term without egress costs eating into margins — R2's zero egress is its killer feature.
avoid_when: You need S3-specific features beyond basic get/put/list, or need AWS-native integrations (Lambda triggers, Athena) that don't work with R2.

auth.notes: R2 API tokens with read/write/admin scopes. S3-compatible auth via access key ID + secret. Workers access R2 bindings natively without additional auth. Account ID required for all operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 10GB storage free/month; 1M Class A operations free/month; 10M Class B operations free/month; ZERO egress fees
pricing.paid_tiers: Storage: $0.015/GB/month; Class A ops (write): $4.50/million; Class B ops (read): $0.36/million
pricing.notes: Zero egress fees is R2's unique advantage vs S3 ($0.09/GB egress). Generous free tier. Requires Cloudflare account with billing setup.

performance.rate_limits: No published rate limits for standard S3 operations; Workers binding: no soft limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent Cloudflare documentation. S3-compatible API means any S3 SDK works. Error responses follow S3 XML format for S3 API; JSON for Cloudflare management API.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: S3 PUT operations are idempotent. Object listing is idempotent. Multipart upload management is idempotent with same upload ID.
known_agent_gotchas: R2 uses S3 API but account ID must be included in the endpoint URL — not standard S3 endpoint format
Object versioning is not supported — R2 does not have S3 versioning; manage versions in object keys
Public bucket access requires explicit public access policy — not enabled by default
R2 does not support S3 events/notifications — cannot trigger Lambda or SNS on object creation
Large object uploads require multipart — single PUT has 5GB limit; multipart required for larger objects

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001. GDPR compliant. Scoped API tokens with read/write/admin granularity. Jurisdiction-specific data location available. Cloudflare's strong security posture.
